articles.title: Assessing Puig Brands (BME:PUIG) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
articles.summary: Recent share performance and business profile Puig Brands (BME:PUIG) has drawn investor attention after a mixed stretch in its share performance, with the stock down about 9% over the past month but up around 4% over the past 3 months. The company operates across fragrance and fashion, makeup, and skincare, generating revenue of €5,042.03m and net income of €593.70m. Its portfolio includes brands such as Carolina Herrera, Jean Paul Gaultier, Charlotte Tilbury, and Uriage, with sales spread...

articles.title: Stocks to Watch Recap: Estee Lauder, IBM, GloubalFoundries
articles.summary: 🔎 Estee Lauder (EL): The U.S. cosmetics giant and Spanish beauty group Puig (ES:PUIG) said they have terminated discussions of a potential business combination, ending speculation that the two could create one of the world’s largest beauty companies.

articles.title: Puig Left Bleeding as Estée Lauder Mega Merger Called Off
articles.summary: The Catalan fragrance empire is forced back to Plan A after the proposed $40 billion marriage comes crashing down.

articles.title: A British Lipstick Queen Derails Estée Lauder’s Big European Deal
articles.summary: Charlotte Tilbury sought to rework her buyout, complicating a fraught deal with Spanish beauty company Puig.

articles.title: Why This Beauty Stock Is the Biggest Riser in the S&P 500 Today
articles.summary: Estée Lauder shares were gaining early on Friday on relief that a prospective merger with Spanish fashion company Puig Brands was off the table. Estée Lauder stock was up 13% in premarket trading, the biggest riser in the Puig Brands was down 14% in Madrid. Estée Lauder slumped when the talks were confirmed in March, with investors fretting over potential share dilution and the complications of acquiring Puig while the company carries out its own turnaround plans.

articles.title: Puig Shares Plunge After Estee Lauder Merger Talks Fail
articles.summary: Shares of Spanish beauty group Puig Brands are tumbling today, following the termination of talks with U.S. cosmetics giant Estee Lauder over a potential merger. Puig's shares were recently down 13% in Europe, on pace for their second-biggest fall since the company started trading in 2024. Estee Lauder's shares, meanwhile, climbed roughly 9% premarket, on track for their best one-day performance in nearly a year.

articles.title: Puig’s Shares Slump After Termination of Merger Talks With Estee Lauder
articles.summary: Madrid-listed shares fell more than 14% in European morning trading, while Estee Lauder’s stock was up over 10% in premarket trading.

articles.title: Estee Lauder Ends Acquisition Talks With Spain’s Puig
articles.summary: The decision ends months of speculation that the two would merge and create one of the world’s largest beauty companies.

articles.title: Estée Lauder shares fall premarket after merger talks update
articles.summary: Investing.com -- Shares of Estée Lauder Companies fell over 2% in premarket trading after Bloomberg reported that the company is advancing in talks over a potential business combination with Puig Brands SA.
